business.headline: Markets open higher as investors await RBI rate decision; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 191.96 points, or 0.26%, to 74,551.97 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 39 points, or 0.17%, to 23,455.55.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall or rise on RBI MPC decision?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,565.00 at 8:17 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 could open above its previous close of 23,416.55.

Gujarat wire-free cities plan: Urban power lines to go underground by 2030Gujarat has approved a statewide mission to move urban power lines underground by 2030. The phased project aims to improve power reliability and safety while making cities less cluttered.

RBI ends FEMA probe against Myntra. Here's what happenedAccording to the Enforcement Directorate (ED), the RBI passed the order on April 20, 2026 under Section 15 of FEMA after receiving a "No Objection" from the agency. As a result, the investigation against the company has been terminated.

LIC owns 10% of Rajesh Exports, shares fall 1% at closing bell amid Sebi allegationsShareholding data for the March 2026 quarter shows that Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC), the country's largest insurer and institutional investor, holds a 10.80% stake in Rajesh Exports.

Revenues are correct, says Rajesh Exports amid Sebi allegationsThe company's response after Sebi issued an interim order against Rajesh Exports and its promoter Rajesh Mehta, raising concerns about the company's financial reporting and questioning whether a large part of its reported revenues had adequate supporting documents.

Sensex, Nifty end flat ahead of RBI policy decision; Titan jumps 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 13.84 points, or 0.02%, to close at 74,360.01, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 10.95 points, or 0.05%, to settle at 23,416.55.

A gold empire, a Swiss refinery and SEBI's Rs 15.15 lakh crore allegationSebi has questioned Rajesh Exports over Rs 15.15 lakh crore of subsidiary revenue between FY21 and FY25. The interim order has intensified scrutiny of the Valcambi owner and sent its shares to the lower circuit.

India plans bond tax break to attract foreign moneyForeign investors have withdrawn nearly $28 billion from Indian equities this year. While foreign money has continued to flow into government bonds, with net investments of around $1.4 billion, policymakers appear keen to attract more stable capital into the debt market.

Why the RBI may avoid a rate cut this time: 3 things to knowAs the Reserve Bank of India prepares to announce its latest monetary policy decision, expectations of another rate cut appear to be fading. Most experts now believe the central bank will keep the repo rate unchanged and continue with a wait-and-watch approach. Here are three reasons why.

Carlsberg India strengthens board as IPO plans gather momentumAccording to officials familiar with the matter, the appointments bring expertise in consumer goods, public policy, leadership and human resources as the company prepares for the next stage of its growth.

How Sebi uncovered a Rs 15 lakh crore revenue hole at Rajesh ExportsA shareholder complaint, a Swiss refinery and several years of unexplained revenues have culminated in one of the most vital accounting investigations in India's corporate history.

Sebi's Rs 15 lakh crore question: What went wrong at Rajesh Exports?Sebi's interim order has questioned whether Rajesh Exports backed its reported revenues with verifiable records. The findings have intensified scrutiny of disclosures, promoter-linked transactions and investor-facing accounts.

Gold is rising again: 3 things investors should knowGold prices are moving higher again after a period of weakness and attracting fresh buying interest. Here's what's driving the rally and what investors should keep in mind.

Rajesh Exports shares hit lower circuit as Sebi alleges 97-99% revenue inflationSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports sent the stock to a 5% lower circuit after it alleged possible revenue inflation and barred promoter Rajesh Mehta. The action has heightened investor uncertainty as the regulator has sought more records and ordered a fresh forensic audit.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran-US tensions keep Dalal Street on edgeIndian benchmark indices opened lower as West Asia tensions and foreign selling weighed on sentiment. Elevated crude prices, FPI outflows and the RBI policy outlook remain key market triggers.

Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

Why file your ITR before July 31? These 3 benefits could helpWith the July 31 ITR deadline approaching, taxpayers have another reason to avoid delays. Filing your return on time can unlock several benefits that could prove useful throughout the year.

India responds to proposed US tariffs as trade pact negotiations continueWhile discussions over the tariff proposal continue, India and the United States are also working to finalise a broader trade agreement.

CCPA fines PhysicsWallah and McAfee for using dark patterns on digital platformsCCPA has also directed both companies to remove dark pattern practices that influenced consumer choices on their digital platforms.

entertainment.headline: The Pyramid Scheme review: A funny, frightening look at Indians' easy-money dreamsRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 TVF's The Pyramid Scheme perfectly captures the struggles of everyday Indians who get lured into MLM traps for quick money. It is funny, deeply relatable, and exposes the ugly truth behind financial scams.

Bandar review: Tough questions and easy answers in Bobby Deol's unsettling filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Bandar movie review: Anurag Kashyap's Bandar follows fading star Samar Mehra (Bobby Deol) as a rape accusation pushes him into police custody and jail. The film dwells on systemic humiliation and prison brutality, even as its moral perspective narrows.

Aamir Khan gave up Bollywood romance 18 years ago. He never gave up on love though Aamir Khan is set to marry partner Gauri Spratt on July 5. The development underlines the contrast between his off-screen life and his long move away from on-screen romance.

Masters of the Universe review: Nicholas Galitzine's He-man is more than muscleRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Masters of the Universe brings He-Man back to theatres as Prince Adam confronts Skeletor and his own doubts. The film reframes the franchise's idea of power through vulnerability, restraint and responsibility.

Amid RJ Balaji's Karuppu row, actor Suriya shares cryptic note on kindness Suriya shared a cryptic Instagram note on love and kindness as Karuppu makers faced criticism over their remarks about Vijay. The post added to a widening debate over credit for the film even as Karuppu continued its strong box-office run.

The Peddi question: Janhvi Kapoor has the projects, where are the performances? Janhvi Kapoor's back-to-back Telugu films with Jr NTR and Ram Charan have renewed questions about her performances and the roles she is taking. The scrutiny deepens in Peddi, where objectification and consent issues overshadow her character.

Months after Trisha row, director Parthiban meets CM Vijay, gifts him painting Filmmaker-actor R Parthiban met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and gifted him a handwritten letter and a custom painting following his election victory. The meeting comes months after Parthiban's remarks about Trisha Krishnan triggered controversy and drew criticism online.

Gullak Season 5 review: New paint on walls, the same warmth withinRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Gullak Season 5 returns to Mishra Niwas as renovation, family shifts and new visitors reshape familiar routines. The season stays gentle and reflective, finding meaning in change, discomfort and old memories.

Peddi box office Day 1: Ram Charan's game-changing Rs 100 crore opener after RRR Peddi box office Day 1: Peddi has given Ram Charan the box-office boost he was looking for. The sports action drama raced past Rs 100 crore worldwide on its opening day.

Six arrested for leaking footage from Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' According to officials, the accused were allegedly involved in sharing and distributing the leaked footage through various digital channels. Authorities said the arrests were part of an ongoing crackdown on those responsible for disseminating the material online.

Did Janhvi Kapoor just like post calling Peddi most expensive disrespect to women? Janhvi Kapoor liked a social media post that called Peddi a disrespectful portrayal of Achiyyamma. The reaction intensified criticism over objectification and the film's consent-free love track.

Shilpa Shinde launches veiled attack on Hina Khan's cancer amid fake harassment row Shilpa Shinde responded after Hina Khan criticised her remarks on the 2016 allegations against Sanjay Kohli. Shinde appeared to reference Khan's illness and family tragedy in the new video.

Chiranjeevi, proud father and actor, reviews Ram Charan's Peddi Chiranjeevi reviewed Ram Charan's sports drama Peddi and congratulated the film's cast and crew after its release. He expressed hope that the film would bring more milestones for Telugu cinema.

Kriti Sanon's trainer on how she achieved her toned look for Cocktail 2 bikini scene Karan Sawhney has detailed how Kriti Sanon prepared for her first bikini scene in Cocktail 2. He highlighted the actor's effort to achieve a fit, lean look without losing the character's glow.

Gram Chikitsalay 2: When and where to watch Amol Parashar's rural drama series Gram Chikitsalay is set to return with a second season as Dr Prabhat's journey in the village of Bhathkandi continues with new challenges. Here's everything to know about the cast, story and where to watch the rural comedy-drama.

Maa Behen actor Dharna reacts to influencers being cast for their social media reach Dharna Durga said she landed Maa Behen after completing the full audition process and later shared screen space with Madhuri Dixit. She said the experience eased doubts around influencer casting and left her impressed by Dixit's warmth.

Actor Sambhavna Seth welcomes twins, says Maha Diwali came early this year Actor Sambhavna Seth and her husband, actor-writer Avinash Dwivedi, have announced the birth of their twins. The announcement marks a joyful turn in their long journey to parenthood and drew warm wishes from fans and celebrities.

Lalit Modi confirms biopic is in the works, says Ranveer Singh wanted to play lead Lalit Modi said his biopic is in the scripting stage. He recalled Ranveer Singh flying to London to discuss playing him on screen.

Ranveer Allahbadia says life won't be same after Ujjain visit with Juhi Bhatt Ranveer Allahbadia celebrated his 33rd birthday with Juhi Bhatt in Ujjain and offered prayers at Mahakaal. The visit also came months after he publicly confirmed their relationship on Instagram.

general news.headline: I'm disappointed: Minister quits DK Shivakumar cabinet over desired ministry snubDenied the Bengaluru Development portfolio and allotted Major and Medium Irrigation instead, Ramalinga Reddy has resigned DK Shivakumar's cabinet.

BJP gives up Andhra Rajya Sabha seat amid K Annamalai exit buzzUnder the revised formula, three of the four Rajya Sabha seats falling vacant in Andhra Pradesh will go to the Telugu Desam Party (TDP), while the remaining seat will be allotted to the Jana Sena Party (JSP).

Shreyas Iyer replaces Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20I captainBCCI begins a new T20I era as Shreyas Iyer replaces World Cup-winning skipper Suryakumar Yadav. After successful IPL stints, Shreyas steps into a leadership role with the national team.

Pause AI development: Anthropic says Claude is improving so fast it may not need humansAnthropic says its Claude AI is no longer just helping humans write code. It is increasingly helping build the next generation of AI systems. The company believes this could dramatically speed up AI development while making human oversight and safety safeguards more important than ever.

Mamata Banerjee's Tollywood brigade missing amid TMC's worst crisisIt's all quiet on the Tollywood front. The Trinamool Congress's usually vocal, star-studded line-up of celebrity MPs and MLAs, who owe their seats to Mamata Banerjee's patronage, have either gone deafeningly silent or distanced themselves from the party as the TMC faces its worst existential crisis.

Don't call yourself TMC: Mahua Moitra rips into 'completely useless' rebel MLAsTMC MP Mahua Moitra accused the BJP of engineering the Trinamool rebellion through coercion and branded the rebel MLAs as "completely useless" leaders who thrived on Mamata Banerjee's charisma but had "lost the stomach" to fight from the opposition benches.

Rebel turns rescuer? Expelled TMC leader offers his seat to Mamata BanerjeeOne of the most influential Muslim leaders, who was once associated with the Trinamool Congress, has offered his seat to Mamata Banerjee so that she can return to the West Bengal Assembly.

Twin fires in Noida: Blaze engulfs 12th floor of high-rise, another at PGA fire broke out in a 12th floor flat in Noida's Ivy County on Friday, while another incident was reported at a PG in Sector 52. Fire teams were sent to both spots, and the causes of the incidents were not immediately known.

What will happen to E20 vehicles when ethanol blending goes beyond 20%?As India prepares for the next phase of ethanol blending, questions are emerging over the future of millions of E20-compatible vehicles and whether they can adapt to higher ethanol concentrations. Let's find out.

After Carlsen, Praggnanandhaa beats Gukesh to go second at Norway ChessR Praggnanandhaa beat World Champion D Gukesh in Round 9 of Norway Chess 2026, moving into second place on 15 points, just half a point behind leader Wesley So. His winning streak has put him in strong title contention.

DU professor found dead in her locked apartment, murder suspectedThe incident came to light at around 2.35 pm when police received a PCR call from her sister, Devarati Paul, informing them that Debosmita had been found dead inside the flat.

The Malviya Nagar fire that exposed Delhi's unregulated medical tourism lodging beltThe tragedy has cast a spotlight on the accommodation ecosystem surrounding Delhi's major hospitals, where patients from India and abroad often stay for weeks or months during treatment.

Bandar review: Tough questions and easy answers in Bobby Deol's unsettling filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026Bandar movie review: Anurag Kashyap's Bandar follows fading star Samar Mehra (Bobby Deol) as a rape accusation pushes him into police custody and jail. The film dwells on systemic humiliation and prison brutality, even as its moral perspective narrows.

6 days with no food or oxygen, missing Sherpa found alive on EverestA missing Nepali Sherpa guide was found alive on Mount Everest after six days. The rescue stunned his grieving family and officials after hope had nearly faded.

He is a good friend: Trump praises PM Modi, signals US-India trade dealUS President Donald Trump praised Prime Minister Narendra Modi and said the US and India would reach a trade deal, citing their strong ties. His remarks came as both sides advanced trade negotiations.

Upset over break-up, man stabs ex-girlfriend inside office; later attempts suicideVictim Dimple was allegedly stabbed by her former partner and colleague, Harjinder Singh Mann. The fatal attack was captured on CCTV cameras installed at their workplace.

The Peddi question: Janhvi Kapoor has the projects, where are the performances?Janhvi Kapoor's back-to-back Telugu films with Jr NTR and Ram Charan have renewed questions about her performances and the roles she is taking. The scrutiny deepens in Peddi, where objectification and consent issues overshadow her character.

Useless to pressure PM Modi, India's US cooperation won't harm Russia ties: PutinVladimir Putin says the US is trying to pressure India over its cooperation with Russia. He says putting pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi is detrimental and unlikely to alter India's pursuit of its national interests.

Choice is yours, Ukraine proposes to end this war: Zelenskyy to PutinIn an open letter to Putin, Zelenskyy said that the conflict has brought mounting costs for both countries and warned that Russia would face growing pressure if the war continues.

A fairytale in Paris: Maja Chwalinska turns struggle into stardom at French OpenFrench Open 2026: Poland's Maja Chwalinska arrived at Roland Garros as a little-known qualifier ranked outside the top 100, but has produced a fairytale run to reach her first Grand Slam final, defeating multiple top-50 players along the way.

Techie regrets quitting MNC for startup, willing to accept pay cut to switch backThe techie shared his experience in a post on the subreddit r/developersIndia titled, "I recently joined a startup and I'm already regretting it. Tips needed to switch back to MNCs."

2000 vs 2026: How the cost of essentials in India has changedComparing selected daily essential items shows how prices have changed between 2000 and 2006, giving a snapshot of increasing household expenses amid India's economic growth.

African couple undergoing IVF died in each other's arms in Delhi hotel fireAn African couple were found dead in each other's arms after succumbing to smoke inhalation in the devastating Malviya Nagar hotel fire that killed 21 people. The couple was undergoing IVF treatment at a Delhi hospital.

Pressure to convert, forced exit: Wipro Pune ex-employee's charges against bossA former Wipro employee in Pune has accused her female boss of mental harassment, religious coercion and forcing her to resign. The company said it is cooperating with investigators and maintains a zero-tolerance policy on workplace misconduct.

sports.headline: After Carlsen, Praggnanandhaa beats Gukesh to go second at Norway Chess R Praggnanandhaa beat World Champion D Gukesh in Round 9 of Norway Chess 2026, moving into second place on 15 points, just half a point behind leader Wesley So. His winning streak has put him in strong title contention.

Once a failed transfer now the head coach, meet Iraola Liverpool's new boss Liverpool have appointed Andoni Iraola as head coach after sacking Arne Slot. The move revives a failed 2014 transfer and signals a reset towards the club's high-intensity identity.

Alexander Zverev eyes final berth; Matteo Arnaldi, Flavio Cobolli carry Italy's hopes Alexander Zverev is two wins away from a maiden Grand Slam title as he targets another French Open final, while rising Czech star Jakub Mensik stands in his path. Meanwhile, Italy is guaranteed a men's singles finalist.

Group C Preview: Neymar's last dance meets Morocco's red-hot challenge Brazil head into Group C as favourites, with Neymar back for another World Cup run. Morocco, Scotland and Haiti ensure the section looks far less straightforward than expected.

A fairytale in Paris: Maja Chwalinska turns struggle into stardom at French Open French Open 2026: Poland's Maja Chwalinska arrived at Roland Garros as a little-known qualifier ranked outside the top 100, but has produced a fairytale run to reach her first Grand Slam final, defeating multiple top-50 players along the way.

Ollie Robinson runs riot as England seize control in Lord's Test vs New Zealand ENG vs NZ, 1st Test: Ollie Robinson took four wickets on his comeback as England reduced New Zealand to 61 for six at Lord's after being bowled out for 140 on a rain-hit opening day in which 16 wickets fell.

Sri Lanka's fast-bowling dropout Rumesh Pathirage joins 90m javelin club Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage, a former fast bowler, set a new meet record in Rome Diamond League with a 92.62m throw on June 4. The 23-year-old became the fourth Asian after Neeraj Chopra, Arshad Nadeem and Cheng Chao-Tsun to breach the 90m mark in men's javelin.

Chwalinska creates history, becomes first qualifier to reach French Open final French Open 2026: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska continued her dream run after beating Diana Shnaider 7-6(4), 6-4 in the women's singles semifinal on Thursday.

Shreyas Iyer replaces Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20I captain BCCI begins a new T20I era as Shreyas Iyer replaces World Cup-winning skipper Suryakumar Yadav. After successful IPL stints, Shreyas steps into a leadership role with the national team.

Pakistan skips Asian Senior Fencing Championships in New Delhi Pakistan has declined India's invitation for the Asian Senior Fencing Championships in New Delhi. The decision comes even as India said Pakistani athletes were welcome at multilateral events.

Wicketkeeper-batter KS Bharat retires at 32 after falling down the pecking order KS Bharat has retired from international cricket after a seven-Test career with India. The move follows his drop in the wicketkeeping pecking order and clears the path for overseas T20 opportunities.

RCB's Jacob Bethell faces the heat as Jamieson blows England away in Lord's TestEngland slid to 118 for 8 on the opening day at Lord's as New Zealand's pacers exposed a brittle batting line-up. Jacob Bethell's modest return intensified questions over his stint in the IPL in the lead-up to the Test series.

Mirra Andreeva heals old wounds, downs Marta Kostyuk to reach maiden major finalFrench Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva continued her dream run at Roland Garros, defeating Marta Kostyuk 6-1, 6-3 to reach her maiden Grand Slam final. The 19-year-old produced a composed and dominant display to end Kostyuk's 17-match clay-court winning streak.

Virat Kohli almost doesn't want the life he lives in India, says ex-RCB teammateFormer England all-rounder Liam Livingstone has revealed that Virat Kohli actively seeks to maintain a low profile in India, shedding light on the sharp contrast between the superstar's ultra-competitive on-field persona and his quiet family life.

Will Rashid Khan return for Tests? Afghanistan captain stays tight-lippedIND vs AFG: Hashmatullah Shahidi declined to clarify whether Rashid Khan will play Test cricket again before Afghanistan's match against India. His absence keeps the leg-spinner's red-ball future uncertain as Afghanistan seek more regular Tests.

Patidar is a strong contender for India's T20I captaincy: Ex-India selectorSaba Karim has put Rajat Patidar in the mix for India's T20I captaincy after his back-to-back IPL titles with RCB. The debate has widened ahead of the Ireland series, with selectors also considering Shreyas Iyer, Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma.

Semi-Finals Highlights: Chwalinska beats Shnaider, sets up final vs AndreevaFrench Open 2026 semifinals Live Updates: Maja Chwalinska has beaten Diana Shnaider in straight sets and has advanced to the final of Roland Garros. Earlier in the day, Mirra Andreeva beat Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to reach her maiden Grand Slam final.

Rohit Sharma doubtful for Afghanistan ODIs after Kohli setbackIndia are awaiting Rohit Sharma's fitness clearance for the Afghanistan ODI series after Virat Kohli was ruled out. The uncertainty has left selectors weighing replacement plans, with Yashasvi Jaiswal in contention and Hardik Pandya also under watch.

Did Dawood Ibrahim plot IPL takeover? Lalit Modi's bombshell claimsFormer IPL chief Lalit Modi has claimed that a plot by Dawood Ibrahim to control a tournament franchise and threats over disrupted illegal betting networks ultimately forced his complete exile from cricket administration.

Gaikwad likely to replace Kohli for Afghanistan ODI seriesVirat Kohli has been ruled out of the Afghanistan ODI series with a hamstring injury from the IPL 2026 final. Ruturaj Gaikwad is the leading candidate to replace him as selectors reshuffle India's squad.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Rose Bowl, SouthamptonThe Rose Bowl, Hampshire's current home ground, opened its doors in 2001 and represented a major step forward for the county's cricket infrastructure. Designed by renowned architects Michael Hopkins & Partners, the venue is known for its bowl-shaped layout and striking three-tier pavilion, which has become a defining feature of the stadium.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford, ManchesterWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford in Manchester is one of England's most historic cricket grounds. Home to Lancashire CCC, it has hosted Test matches since 1884 and is known for its electric atmosphere. Old Trafford can accommodate up to 26,000 spectators.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Headingley, LeedsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Headingley is one of England's most historic cricket grounds, famed for the 1981 Ashes miracle and generations of iconic performances. The Leeds venue remains a stage for unforgettable cricket moments.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Edgbaston, BirminghamWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 venue: Edgbaston is widely regarded as one of cricket's premier venues and has served as the home ground of Warwickshire County Cricket Club since 1885.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 venue: Lord's, LondonLord's will stage key Women's T20 World Cup matches, including the final in London. The venue's history, slope and intense atmosphere add extra weight to every contest.

How Gill became India's new Test run machine: Ashwin reveals technique tweakAshwin said Shubman Gill's tighter bat-pad alignment has transformed his Test batting. He believes the change can define India's batting line-up for years and score more runs at home.

Sai Sudharsan vs Devdutt Padikkal: India's pick for No.3 to get a long ropeIND vs AFG: India's search for a permanent Test No. 3 continues. Coach Ryan ten Doeschate promises a long rope for Devdutt Padikkal or Sai Sudharsan ahead of the Afghanistan clash.

Rishabh Pant doesn't need a title to lead, he is in great spirits: India coachRyan ten Doeschate said Rishabh Pant remains upbeat and influential despite losing India's vice-captaincy for the Afghanistan Test. India will play the one-off Test in New Chandigarh from June 6.

Ayush Shetty falls to Lee Chiuk Yiu as India crash out of Indonesia OpenAyush Shetty lost to Lee Chiuk Yiu in three games, ending India's last live run at the Indonesia Open. The defeat completed a poor week for the Indian big names, with Sindhu beaten, others out early and Sat-Chi withdrawing injured.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
